content.text:
Sheet: Sheet1 ================================================== Section                                                                                             Question                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Answer 1. General                                                                                  What is BatchSmart?                                                                                   BatchSmart is an AI-powered tool that helps pharmaceutical manufacturers review their paper-based Batch Manufacturing Records (BMRs) faster and more accurately. Instead of a person manually reading every handwritten entry on a batch record and checking it against the rules, BatchSmart reads the scanned document with AI, checks the values automatically, and highlights anything that looks wrong or needs a second look. Who is BatchSmart for?                                                                                             Three main groups use it:
 - **Batch Release & Quality team members** — the people who review completed batch records before a batch can be released.
 - **QA Managers** — who oversee the review process, resolve flagged issues, and make sure records are audit-ready.
 - **Corporate Quality Leaders** — who oversee quality across multiple plants/facilities and need a bird's-eye view of how things are going. Do I need to be technical to use it?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       No. Day-to-day use is upload, review, comment, and approve — similar to reviewing a document in any office software. Some setup tasks (like configuring a new document template) are done by an admin, but reviewing and approving batches doesn't require any technical skill. What problem is BatchSmart actually solving?                                                                                                                                       Reviewing paper batch records by hand is slow and error-prone — a person has to cross-check every handwritten number, checkbox, and signature against the approved procedure. BatchSmart automates that comparison so human reviewers can focus their attention on the handful of things that genuinely need judgment, instead of re-checking everything from scratch.
 
 --- 2. The Basics: MBR vs BMR                                                      What's the difference between an MBR and a BMR? - **MBR (Master Batch Record)** is the *template* — the official, approved procedure for manufacturing a product, including every field that needs to be filled in, the acceptable ranges, and the checkboxes and sign-off points.
 - **BMR (Batch Manufacturing Record)** is the *filled-in copy* for one specific production run — the actual paper record an operator wrote on while making that batch.
 
 Think of the MBR as a blank form with rules, and the BMR as one completed, signed copy of that form. Why does the MBR need to be set up before I can upload a BMR?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   BatchSmart checks the BMR against the MBR's rules — its expected ranges, required checkboxes, formulas, and signature points. Without an approved MBR on file, there's nothing to compare the batch record against. Who sets up the MBR?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Usually an admin or QA lead uploads the MBR once per product/procedure and defines its fields and rules. Regular reviewers typically don't need to do this — they just upload BMRs against an MBR that's already been approved.
 
 --- 3. Uploading and Processing a BMR                                                              How do I get started reviewing a batch?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    You upload the scanned/photographed batch record (the BMR) against the matching, already-approved MBR. BatchSmart takes it from there — reading the pages, extracting the values, and comparing them to the rules. How long does it take to process a batch record?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     It depends on the number of pages, but the AI processing (reading text, checkboxes, signatures, and running the checks) typically happens in the background within a few minutes of upload. You'll see the record's status change as it moves through processing. What do the different statuses mean?                                                                                                                   - **Not Started** — uploaded but processing hasn't begun yet.
 - **Processing** — BatchSmart is currently reading and checking the pages.
 - **Completed** — processing finished; results are ready to review.
 - **Error** — something went wrong on a page (e.g., an unreadable scan) and needs attention.
 - **Released** — the batch has been reviewed and formally signed off as complete. What if my scan quality is poor?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Very blurry, skewed, or low-resolution scans can make it harder for the AI to read values accurately, which usually shows up as more "low confidence" flags or missed values (see below). Clear, straight, well-lit scans give the most reliable results.
 
 --- 4. Understanding Discrepancies                                                                             What is a "discrepancy"?                                                                                                                                                                                                     A discrepancy is anything BatchSmart flags as not matching what was expected — for example, a number outside its allowed range, a missing entry, a wrong checkbox selection, or a signature that doesn't line up with where one was expected. It's the AI equivalent of a reviewer circling something in red pen. What kinds of discrepancies will I see?                                                        - **Missing Values** — a field that should have an entry but appears blank.
 - **Data Discrepancy** — a value that's outside its allowed range, doesn't match an expected number/text/date, or fails a calculation check.
 - **In-Process Checks Discrepancy** — a checkbox or selection that doesn't match one of the allowed options.
 - **Signature Discrepancy** — an expected signature that's missing, unclear, or doesn't match the expected signer. What does "low confidence" mean?                                                                                                                                           It means the AI wasn't fully sure it read a value correctly — even if the value itself looks like it's within range. It's a "please double-check this" flag rather than a hard error. This commonly happens with unclear handwriting, faint ink, or unusual formatting. Low-confidence items are shown separately so reviewers know exactly where to focus their attention. I saw a value get "auto-corrected" — can I trust it?                                                                                               Sometimes the AI recognizes that a value is probably slightly misread (for example, a digit that looks like it could be a "0" or an "8") and self-corrects it if the corrected version fits the rules. Whenever this happens, BatchSmart always marks that value as low confidence, specifically so a human reviewer double-checks it before it's trusted. Auto-correction is a helpful suggestion, not a final answer. What happens with corrected or crossed-out handwriting on the original paper record?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     BatchSmart detects strikethroughs (where an operator crossed out and corrected an entry, a normal and compliant practice on paper records) so that the crossed-out value isn't mistakenly read as the real entry. Can I dismiss or remove a discrepancy if it's a false alarm?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 Yes — reviewers can remove a discrepancy once they've confirmed it isn't a real issue (for example, if the AI misread something but the original entry is actually correct).
 
 --- 5. Checkboxes and Signatures                                           How does BatchSmart handle checkboxes on the batch record?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            It detects which boxes were marked and how (a tick, a cross, or filled in solid), then checks whether the marked option is one of the allowed choices for that field. If an unexpected option was selected, it's flagged as a discrepancy. How does signature checking work?                                                                                                                                                                                                                        BatchSmart looks for a signature in the specific spot on the page where one is expected (e.g., an operator or QA sign-off box) and gives it a confidence score based on how clearly a signature is present. If a required signature appears to be missing or unclear, it's flagged for review. Does BatchSmart identify *whose* signature it is?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            It can match signatures to known employees where that mapping has been set up, which helps confirm the right person signed off at the right step.
 
 --- 6. Remarks, Approvals, and Releasing a Batch                                                                                  What is a "remark"?                                                                                                                                                                                                   A remark is a note a reviewer adds to a flagged field — explaining the discrepancy, providing context, or recording a decision (e.g., "confirmed correct, AI misread handwriting"). Remarks can be marked with a priority (high/medium/low) and go through their own approval status (pending → approved/rejected). Who approves remarks?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Typically a QA Manager or another authorized reviewer, depending on how your organization's approval process is set up. What does "Releasing" a BMR mean?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Releasing is the final step that formally signs off a batch record as reviewed and complete. Only users with the right permission can release a batch, and it usually happens only after all discrepancies have been addressed. What are "approval flows"?                                                                                                                                                                                                          Some organizations require more than one person to sign off before an MBR or a BMR review is finalized — for example, a QA Manager and then a QA Head. Approval flows let admins configure that multi-step sign-off chain so the system enforces the right people review things in the right order.
 
 --- 7. Dashboards and Reports                                                                      What can I see on my dashboard?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               An overview of batch activity — how many batches are in progress, completed, or flagged with discrepancies, along with charts breaking this down (e.g., by department or process step). I work across multiple plants/facilities — can I see them all in one place?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Yes. Corporate Quality Leaders get rolled-up views across facilities and sub-companies, so you don't have to check each plant separately. What is APQR?                                                                                                                                                                                                                         APQR-style reports let you track a specific measurement (e.g., a fill weight or yield) across many batches of the same product over time — showing minimums, maximums, averages, and any batches that deviated from expectations. It's useful for spotting trends rather than one-off issues. Can I compare two versions of a document?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Yes — there's a document comparison tool that highlights exactly what changed between two PDFs (added, removed, or modified content), which is useful when checking a revised MBR against a prior version.
 
 --- 8. Roles and Access                                   Why can't I see certain buttons or pages that a colleague can see?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Access in BatchSmart is role-based — what you can view or do (e.g., approve an MBR, release a BMR, manage users) depends on your assigned role and permissions. If something feels like it's missing, it's likely a permissions setting your admin controls, not a bug. What are the typical roles in BatchSmart?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     Roles range from everyday reviewers up through QA Managers, QA/IPQA leads, plant admins, and company-wide admins, reflecting your organization's own hierarchy (Company → Sub-Company → Plant/Facility).
 
 --- 9. Data, Security, and Compliance                                                                                   Is my data secure?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Yes — BatchSmart can be deployed in the cloud or on-premises depending on your organization's requirements, and access to records is controlled by user roles and permissions. Is there an audit trail?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 Yes — user actions are logged, which supports the audit-readiness that regulated pharmaceutical environments require. Where is my data stored?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     Depending on how your organization has deployed BatchSmart, data can be stored locally/on-premises or in cloud storage. This is a deployment choice made by your organization, not something individual users configure.
 
 --- 10. Credits and Subscription                                                                                  What are "credits"?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              BatchSmart usage (processing pages/documents with AI) is metered using credits, similar to a usage-based subscription. Your plan determines how many you have available. What happens if we run low on credits?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Processing new documents may be limited until more credits are added or your subscription is upgraded. An admin can request a plan change or additional credits — this isn't something an individual reviewer needs to manage.
 
 --- 11. Troubleshooting I got a message that a page "can't be processed right now" or "is in process" — what does that mean?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           It usually means that page is currently being reprocessed (e.g., using a more powerful AI model for a difficult page) or is temporarily locked because another user is actively working on it. Wait a moment and try again. I got an error about a page range or user assignment — what should I check?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       These messages usually mean the start/end page numbers overlap with another assignment, or a start page is listed after an end page. Double-check the page numbers you entered. The system says only PDF/image files are allowed — why?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      BatchSmart only processes document scans and images (PDF, JPG, PNG, etc.), since it needs to visually read the page content. Other file types (like Word documents) aren't supported for upload. Who do I contact if something looks wrong and I can't resolve it myself?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Reach out to your internal BatchSmart admin or QA lead first, since many settings (user access, MBR configuration, credits) are managed at that level rather than by individual users.
